scope.area: main process ProcessSupervisor and forked process lifecycle

summary.highLevelVerdict: Ready: supervisor bootstrap now waits for RPC-router readiness before launches proceed, child registration and teardown are guarded, shutdown waits for async exit cleanup, heartbeat-based reload logic is strong, reload failure paths now settle into explicit CRASH and DEAD reporting, and main-process shutdown protection is installed before children are spawned. Missing/problematic: supervisor-visible child state reporting is still thin because children only publish ready over the state channel, leaving shutdown and crash lifecycle visibility mostly local to each process. Optional follow-up: richer supervision telemetry and end-to-end tests would make the process layer easier to audit under failure.

supervisorBootstrapAndRouting.question: Is the supervisor now ready before child launches rely on RPC routing and state listeners?
supervisorBootstrapAndRouting.answer: Yes. The constructor captures RPC router setup into rpcRouterReadyPromise, #setRpcRouter installs state and heartbeat listeners, and launchProcess() explicitly waits for that promise before spawning any child. This removes the earlier startup-race concern and keeps label, pid, and RPC routing registration aligned with listener readiness.

evidence.note: RPC router setup is captured immediately into rpcRouterReadyPromise during construction.
The supervisor bootstrap path registers the current process and installs state and heartbeat listeners.
launchProcess() waits for supervisor readiness before any spawn occurs.
Successful launches register routing targets and initialize health tracking only after adapter setup succeeds.

itemsToGrantCorrectness.item: Child launches wait for supervisor RPC-router readiness
Supervisor state and heartbeat listeners are installed before child launches proceed
Children become addressable by label and pid after successful launch

launchAndExitLifecycle.question: Do launch, rollback, exit, and shutdown flows now complete with the right cleanup boundaries?
launchAndExitLifecycle.answer: Yes. Constructor failures and post-spawn setup failures still run launch error handling and cleanup, failed launches are rolled back out of routing state, exit handling performs hooks and cleanup before resolving exitTeardownPromise, and shutdownProcess() waits for that teardown promise before reporting success.

evidence.note: Constructor failures route through launch error handling and cleanup.
Exit handling runs exit hooks, unregisters routing, performs cleanup tasks, and resolves exitTeardownPromise at the end.
shutdownProcess() waits on exitTeardownPromise and only reports success after the teardown path settles.
Failed launches are rolled back out of maps, routing, and transient health state before cleanup.

itemsToGrantCorrectness.item: Failed launches are rolled back out of routing and supervisor state
Exit cleanup completes before shutdown resolves success
shutdownAllChildren reflects real per-child shutdown results

heartbeatAndReloadSafety.question: Are heartbeat supervision and reload/restart flows now self-consistent and terminally explicit?
heartbeatAndReloadSafety.answer: Yes overall. The supervisor tracks health snapshots and timeout windows per child, triggers reloads for unhealthy or silent children, rebuilds fresh process options on relaunch, and now emits CRASH plus a final DEAD when intentional reload relaunch fails or when sending the supervisor exit command fails. This area is now materially safer than in the earlier snapshots.

evidence.note: Newly launched children immediately enter heartbeat timeout tracking.
Heartbeat payloads are validated, stored, and can trigger reloads when unhealthy.
Reload flow rebuilds fresh process options and now emits CRASH plus DEAD on relaunch failure paths.

itemsToGrantCorrectness.item: Heartbeat health is tracked per child process
Silent or unhealthy children trigger supervised reload behavior
Failed reload paths settle into explicit CRASH and DEAD reporting

mainProcessExitSafety.question: Does the main process install coordinated shutdown protection before the supervised tree is launched?
mainProcessExitSafety.answer: Yes. Main binds BootResolver exit handlers before children are started, registers a shutdown task that asks the supervisor to stop all children, and only then launches manager, engines, and tenant auto-spawn routing. BootResolver also handles supervisor commands, disconnect, signals, beforeExit, uncaughtException, and unhandledRejection centrally.

evidence.note: Main installs exit handlers and child-shutdown protection before launching the child tree.
Manager, engines, and tenant temporary spawner registration all happen after shutdown protection is in place.
Supervisor commands, disconnect, SIGINT, SIGTERM, and beforeExit all trigger coordinated shutdown work.
uncaughtException and unhandledRejection emit local process error/crash hooks before fatal exit.

itemsToGrantCorrectness.item: Main has child-shutdown protection before the first child is spawned
Fatal runtime failures trigger coordinated shutdown work
Tenant auto-spawn registration happens only after supervisor and shutdown wiring are in place

childStateReportingAndObservability.question: Does the supervisor receive rich child lifecycle state updates beyond initial readiness?
childStateReportingAndObservability.answer: Only partially. The supervisor can react to ready, shutdown, and crash state messages, but the current child bootstraps only send ready over the state channel. Shutdown and crash lifecycle visibility therefore exists mainly as local process hooks inside each child, not as explicit supervisor-visible state transitions.

evidence.note: The supervisor has hook routing for ready, shutdown, and crash state messages.
Engine reports heartbeat and ready state, but does not send shutdown or crash state messages.
Manager reports heartbeat and ready state only.
Tenant app reports heartbeat and ready state only.

itemsToGrantCorrectness.item: Children actively publish ready state to the supervisor
Children actively publish shutdown and crash states to the supervisor

shouldFix: Have children publish shutdown and crash state messages to the supervisor instead of exposing those transitions only through local process hooks.
Add end-to-end supervision tests for launch rollback, graceful shutdown timeout, heartbeat-driven reload, and reload-relaunch failure.
niceToHave: Surface richer child lifecycle telemetry in logs or CLI tooling so ready, shutdown, crash, and restart history can be audited more easily.
